servinfo.title: Front Brake Pad Removal and Installation

attention4.ptxt: Failure to take proper precautions during front brake caliper removal may cause damage to the front brake flexible hose and/or the brake piston.

item.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the front brake flexible hose during front brake pad removal.
Avoid depressing the brake pedal during the front brake pad removal procedure to prevent the brake piston from being forced out of the cylinder.
Check for leakage of brake fluid when removing front brake pads and repair front brake caliper if leakage is found.
Replace all brake pads at the same time even if only one pad is defective.

s1.ptxt: Hoist vehicle and remove front wheel.
Remove front brake caliper pin bolts (1).
Remove front brake caliper (1) from front brake caliper carrier (2).

attention4.ptxt: If the removed front brake caliper (1) is left hung by the front brake flexible hose, the front brake flexible hose may be damaged due to the tension caused by the caliper’s weight.
Hang a removed front brake caliper with a wire hook (4) or the like to relieve the front brake flexible hose of the tension.

s1.ptxt: Remove front brake pads (3) and pad springs (5) from front brake caliper carrier (2).

attention4.ptxt: If the front brake flexible hose is twisted, the hose will be damaged.
If the front brake flexible hose is twisted, reinstall front brake caliper being careful not to let front brake flexible hose get twisted.

s1.ptxt: Install pad springs (1) to front brake caliper carrier (2).
Fit new shims (1) to inner brake pad (2) and outer brake pad (3).
Install front brake pads (1) with shims to front brake caliper carrier (2) as shown in figure.

attention3.ptxt: Be sure to install inner pad at inside of caliper carrier with its wear indicator (1) positioned upside.

s1.ptxt: Install front brake caliper and tighten caliper pin bolts (1) to specified torque.
Make sure that brake system is operating properly.
